texts.full_text: adults before and six months after gastric bypass surgery in Roux-en-Y (RYGB) and evaluate the doses of calcium and vitamin D supplementation after surgery. Retrospective longitudinal study of adult patients of both sexes undergoing RYGB. We obtained data on weight, height, BMI and serum concentrations of 25-hydroxyvitamin D, ionized calcium and PTH. Following surgery, patients received dietary supplementation daily 500 mg calcium carbonate and 400 IU vitamin D. We studied 56 women and 27 men. Preoperative serum concentrations of vitamin D were inadequate in 45% of women and 37% of men, while in the postoperative period, these levels worsened significantly with over 89% showing insufficient vitamin D status at six months after surgery despite supplementation. The study highlights a significant issue where routine supplementation may not be sufficient to maintain adequate nutritional status following Roux-en-Y gastric bypass (RYGB) surgery. The primary findings suggest that conventional doses of calcium and vitamin D are inadequate for maintaining proper serum levels post-surgery, which could have implications on bone health due to potential deficiencies in these nutrients. Further investigation into higher supplementation regimes or alternative forms of nutrient delivery might be necessary after RYGB procedures to mitigate risks associated with osteoporosis and other related complications. This includes the consideration of more frequent monitoring and tailored supplement regimens based on individual patient needs post-surgery, as nutritional requirements may vary widely among patients due to differences in dietary habits and pre-existing deficiencies. These results underscore the importance of ongoing research into long-term management strategies

texts.full_text: ase Control and Prevention (CDC). Risk factors for drug-resistant pneumococcal infection were evaluated. Hospital laboratories in the United States submitting pneumococcal isolates to the CDC between October 1, 1991, and September 30, 1992. A total of 544 persons with pneumococci isolated from normally sterile sites. A total of 13 hospitals in 12 states actively participated in an ongoing pneumococcal surveillance study. Resistance to penicillin was detected in 6.6% of isolates, including 1.3% of isolates with MICs of 2.0 micrograms/mL or more (compared with < 0.02% of isolates with MIC > or = 2.0 micrograms/mL identified by CDC surveillance from 1979 to 1987). A total of 16.4% were resistant to at least one of the following drugs or drug classes: penicillin, cephalosporins, macrolides, combination trimethoprim and sulfamethoxazole, and chloramphenicol. Six serotypes (6B, 23F, 14, 9V,...and 19A) were responsible for a majority of the drug-resistant pneumococcal infections. The majority of the isolates were from patients with underlying conditions such as HIV/AIDS, cancer, or immunosuppression. Patients who received prolonged courses of antibiotics and those with chronic medical conditions were also found to be at higher risk

texts.full_text: Inter-individual variation in DNA methylation extent has been associated with increased risk for many chronic diseases including cancer \[[@B1]-[@B4]\]. Global DNA hypomethylation, the genome-wide loss of methylcytosine, has been observed in malignant and benign tumors and normal tissues surrounding tumors, indicating that global DNA hypomethylation may be one of the early molecular events in carcinogenesis \[[@B5]-[@B7]\]. This reduced global DNA methylation impacts repetitive DNA sequences rich in CpG dinucleotides such as long interspersed nucleotide element-1 (LINE-1). Methylation extent at LINE-1 sequences, as a surrogate marker of global DNA methylation status, varies by gender, age and environmental and lifestyle factors \[[@B3],[@B8]-[@B14]\]. Methylation of DNA requires the methyl donor *S*-adenosylmethionine (SAM), a key metabolite in on...one-carbon metabolism. The methylation cycle is fueled by dietary intake of one-carbon metabolites, such as folate and choline \[[@B15]\]. Therefore, nutritional status can influence DNA methylation extent via its effect on the availability of methyl donors or cofactors for transmethylation reactions. For example, reduced folate intake has been associated with lower DNA methylation in healthy individuals \[[@B16]-[@B18]\], while higher choline intake is related to increased LINE-1 methylation in a large population study \[[@B19]\]. However, little is known about the effect of dietary protein on DNA methylation. Dietary protein is digested into peptides and amino acids

texts.full_text: bug, which yields a red color eight times brighter than kermes. The Spaniards valued this insect, developing and marketing its carmine red pigment. The word carmine, used specifically for this color, is derived from the similarity of cochineal to kermes, which it eventually replaced as the most common color. One of the common uses of this dye is in bright red punch, which, for this reason, became commonly called in camps “bug juice.” KOSHER CARMINE? Are kermes and carmine kosher for food coloring? Whether kermes and carmine pigments are kosher or not depends on why some contend that kermes could be used to dye the garments of the kohanim. Let us review the four answers that I quoted above and see how each one impacts our shaylah. Approach (1) above permitted dyeing a mitzvah item using non-kosher material, since the latter is not the primary item, but oNly a coloring. This approach would prohibit use of color from a non-kosher source in a product that one intends to eat. Approach (2) ruled that mitzvah items that do not contain Hashem’s name or a holy verse may be produced from non-kosher substances. This reason would also forbid use of kermes or carmine colors for food. Nevertheless, both the third and fourth approaches mentioned would permit using cochineal coloring in a kosher product. From 2007 to 2009, after extending his contract, de la Peña was severely hindered with injury problems. However, on 21 February 2009, he managed to net twice in a 2–1 derby win at Barça, with one side ranking first in the league and the other last; Espanyol eventually finished in 10th position. After the shocking death of new team captain Daniel Jarque, in August 2009, de la Peña was named as his replacement. His physical problems continued to bother him tremendously, to a point which he said that if this became a major issue he would retire, which happened on 22 May 2011 at the age of 35, immediately after a home match against Sevilla. International De la Peña, alongside the likes of Gaizka Mendieta, Fernando Morientes and Raúl, played for the Spanish under-21s at both the 1996 UEFA European Championship and the 1996 Summer Olympics, helping the nation reach the quarter-finals in the latter. Despite this, he did not make his debut for the senior team until 9 February 2005, at the age of 28 years and 9 months in a 2006 FIFA World Cup qualifier against San Marino (Almería, 5–0). He subsequently played for Spain on five occasions during the year, with his last coming against the same opponent. Post-retirement On 8 June 2011, de la Peña was announced as part of the new coaching staff that would work alongside new manager Luis Enrique – a former teammate at Barcelona – at Serie A club A.S. Roma. It was announced in August that he would be taking a leave of absence for family reasons. This page offers a large list of options. How to Use Firebug When you launch Firebug on a web page, it opens up in its own window and displays the HTML code for that particular webpage. You'll see a panel with multiple tabs: HTML, CSS, Script, Console, DOM, Net (Network), and more. Each tab provides different information about the current state of your website or application: - HTML Tab: This is where you can inspect the HTML structure of the page. By clicking on any element in this view, Firebug highlights it within the actual webpage, making it easy to understand how the code corresponds to what's visible onscreen. You also have options for editing attributes and styles directly from here without affecting the original source file. - CSS Tab: Here you can see all CSS rules applied to the selected HTML element or page a

texts.full_text: specifications for image encoding schemes, can use different quantization steps for transform coefficient components to quantize image data in a profile to High Profile or higher. A quantization step for each transform coefficient component can be configured based on a quantization matrix (also referred to as a scaling list) and a reference step value. The quantization matrix is defined as a size substantially the same as an orthogonal transform FIG. 19 illustrates preset values (default values) for four types of quantization matrices predefined in H.264/AVC. For example, matrix SL01 is a default for the quantization matrix if the transform unit size is 4×4 in prediction mode. Matrix SL02 is a default for the quantization matrix if the transform unit size is 4×4 in inter prediction mode. Matrix SL03 is a default for the quantization matrix if the transform unit size is 8×8 in intra prediction mode. Matrix SL04 is a default for the quantization matrix if the transform unit size is 8×8 in inter prediction mode. A user can use a sequence parameter set or a picture parameter set to specify a specific quantization matrix different from the default values shown in FIG. 19. If the quantization matrix is not used, an equal value is used for all components of the quantization step used for the quantization. texts.full_text: basis I need to open files that have really long lines, which unfortunately causes emacs to grind to a halt. My understanding is that this is a long-term issue with the renderer. I've tried disabling every possible mode, using every possible line wrapping package, etc. but none work well enough. What I have found does work is first transforming the files in shell before opening them: $ fold -w80 myfile > temp generally speaking I don't need to edit the files that have this property just view them, but it would still be convenient to look at them in emacs insteadA: You can create a custom Emacs function or command to pipe large files through 'fold' before opening. Here's an example of how you could do it using Emacs Lisp: ```lisp (defun fold-and-open (file width) "Pipe FILE through fold with WIDTH and open the result in Emacs." (interactive (list (read-file-name "File to pipe: " nil nil t))) ; Prompt for file, allow completion & default is arg ;; Create a temporary buffer name using current time as it's unlikely two files will be opened at exactly same second (let ((temp-buffer-name (concat "*folded-" (number-to-string (+ (* 10000 (- (current-time) 3600))) ; Convert to milliseconds, then remove hours (nth 2 (decode-time)))))) ;; Run the shell command (shell-command (concat "fold -w" width " '" file "' > temp")) ;; Open the temporary buffer with content from 'temp' and kill it when done. (find-file (kill-buffer nil))) ``` Now, you can call this function interactively by running `M-x fold-and-open RET`, Emacs will prompt for a file name to process, the default width is 80 characters but you may change it as needed. This should help you efficiently view large files with long lines in Emacs without causi

John Fisher, head of the Marine and Industrial division at Long Painting, spoke about the decision to move to the islands. “We went to Hawaii back in 1990 at the request of a major customer, Kiewit Construction,” Fisher said. “They contracted us to help finish a massive project – the Waikoloa Hyatt Regency on the Big Island.” Over one mile long and boasting 1200 rooms, this high-level resort was a major project for Long Painting. “We had an average of about 25 people working on Waikoloa and by the end we ramped up to about 35 people,” Fisher said. “This was a brand new resort and we did all the wall coverings, painting, reflecting pools, fountains and painting on the interior and exterior, restaurants, guest rooms etc.” the Waikoloa Hyatt Regency provided Long painting the opportunity to get a foot in the door to the Hawaiian market, and while there, the company was able to pick another major project working on the Hyatt Regency in Kauai. “It was in 1991 that we decided to make the move and build up a little office in Honolulu to take care of the Hawaiian market,” Fisher said. “We had a six man group running the office down there full time and were doing $4-5 million worth of work annually.” While Long Painting stayed busy working several resorts at a time, Fisher said the company received major push back from the local contractors in the area. “It was a real task to be out there,” fisher said. “If you’re not from there the locals let you know it. For the bigger projects, we had to hire about 60-70% of our outside labor from the mainland in order to finish some of these major resorts on time.” Long Painting worked on four of the biggest four-star resorts during the construction boom, and was able to supplement smaller painting jobs in the interim, such as schools and municipal buildings. texts.full_text: year were adequate savings, but I didn't. However, as far back as I can remember, which goes into the 1960's, SS was a "lockbox" for workers' taxes, protected from the whims of politics and ensuring that future generations would receive benefits. But let's fast forward to the 1980s. The economy was in a different place, and so was the political climate. Inflation was on the rise, and so was the call for "reform." The idea of privatizing Social Security was floated, and with it, the promise of higher returns and personal investment accounts. The political rhetoric was all about choice and freedom, and the "lockbox" was opened. In 1983, a deal was struck. The Greenspan Commission, chaired by then-Chairman of the Federal Reserve Alan Greenspan, was tasked with fixing the looming SS crisis. They recommended increasing the payroll tax and adjusting the retirement age to prevent the system from running out of money. The system was made solvent for the next 75 years, and the narrative shifted to one of bipartisan success. But the "fix" was only temporary. The system was never truly reformed; it was merely patched up. The payroll tax increase was temporary, and the retirement age adjustment was minimal. The system was still underfunded, and the "lockbox" was only as good as the political will to keep its promises. Fast forward to today, and the "lockbox" is more myth than reality. The money that was supposed to be set aside for future retirees has been siphoned off for other government spending. The "lockbox" has been opened, and the money has been used to fund other government programs, leaving future retirees at the mercy of the stock market and the whims of politicians. The lesson here is not just about the history of Social Security, but about the dangers of political promises and the importance of financial transparency. texts.full_text: he Empty Hanger is a workshop based project that runs in schools, retreats, youth groups and colleges. Created at London college of Fashion, the Empty Hanger is a project that aims to address the issue of clothing waste and promote sustainable fashion. The project is based on the concept of a clothing swap, where participants bring in clothing that they no longer wear and swap it with the clothing of others. The Empty Hanger is a creative and engaging way to promote sustainable fashion and to encourage participants to think about the environmental and social impact of the fashion industry. The project is based on the idea that clothing is a valuable and valuable resource that should be used and reused to the fullest. texts.full_text: step-by-step: ### Using SQL Commands 1. Identify the Foreign Key Constraint: First, you need to know the name of the foreign key constraint that references the parent table. You can find this information by querying the data dictionary views like `USER_CONSTRAINTS` and `USER_CON_CONSTRAINTS`. 2. Drop the Existing Foreign Key Constraint (if necessary): If the foreign key already exists without the `ON DELETE CASCADE` option, you need to drop it first. ```sql ALTER TABLE child_table DROP CONSTRAINT fk_constraint_name; ``` 3. Add the Foreign Key Constraint with ON DELETE CASCADE: Now, add the foreign key constraint with the `ON DELETE CASCADE` option. ```sql ALTER TABLE child_table ADD CONSTRAINT fk_constraint_name FOREIGN KEY (child_column) REFERENCES parent_table(parent_column) ON DELETE CASCADE; ``` ### Using SQL Developer GUI 1. This study builds upon our original study by providing an understanding of the etiology of the underlying modelling errors through comparison of the muscle activation predictions against EMG. The next stages of this process require an adaption of the musculoskeletal models to e.g., improve lever arms of the gluteal muscles *etc*,[@CR32] and investigate whether the reported errors can be mitigated. This continuing process highlights the importance of benchmarking musculoskeletal modeling techniques using multiple subjects and activities.[@CR43] This study provides a demonstration of the comprehensive validation of musculoskeletal model predictions enabled through the CAMS-Knee datasets. However, the datasets are still limited to six elderly patients with total knee replacements. Thus, it remains unknown whether the presented limitations of musculoskeletal model predictions can be extrapolated to healthy subjects or patients with pathologic knee conditions. texts.full_text: k. This is Walltown, Northumberland, the former quarry, then Walltown Crags and a section of Hadrian's Wall.The landscape was breathtaking, even under the blanket of snow. The remnants of the ancient wall stood as a testament to time, stretching across the rolling hills. The cold air was crisp and refreshing, a stark contrast to the indoor warmth we had grown accustomed to. The walk was invigorating, despite the chilly weather. The snow crunched under our boots, and the silence was occasionally broken by the distant sound of birds or the rustling of trees. The quarry at Walltown was a sight to behold, its rugged cliffs and deep crevices adding a dramatic touch to the landscape. As we continued our trek, we reached Walltown Crags, where the remnants of Hadrian's Wall offered a glimpse into the past. The stonework, though weathered by time, still held an air of grandeur. it was a humbling experience to stand where ancient Romans once stood, guarding the northern frontier of their empire. The walk was not just a physical exercise but also a mental one. The tranquility of the surroundings allowed for reflection and a deeper appreciation of nature and history. It was a reminder that sometimes, the best way to recharge is to step away from the screen and immerse oneself
